title: Fix the Method Rename Cascade
description: A method was renamed and its parameter order changed, breaking subclasses and callers.
pr_diff:
PR Diff:
  - def send_notification(user_id: int, message: str) -> bool:
  + def dispatch_alert(message: str, user_id: int, priority: str = "normal") -> bool:
initial_ci_output:
[FAILED] mypy
error: Module has no attribute "send_notification" [attr-error] (x3)

[FAILED] unit_tests
AttributeError: module has no attribute 'send_notification'

[FAILED] integration
AssertionError: notification was not dispatched

files.src/notifier.py:
def dispatch_alert(message: str, user_id: int, priority: str = "normal") -> bool:
    return True
files.src/handler.py:
from src.notifier import send_notification
send_notification(user_id=42, message="hello")
files.src/scheduler.py:
from src.notifier import send_notification
result = send_notification(1, "scheduled task")
files.tests/test_notifier.py:
def test_send():
    assert send_notification(1, "test") is True
files.tests/test_integration.py:
result = send_notification(user_id=1, message="integration")
assert result is True

full_suite.failure_output: full suite still references old send_notification API
